name: Total number of movies in the dataset
query:
MATCH (m:Movie)
RETURN count(m) AS total_movies;

name: Total number of ratings
query:
MATCH ()-[r:RATED]->()
RETURN count(r) AS total_ratings;

name: Top 10 most-rated movies
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
RETURN m.movieId AS movieId,
       m.title AS title,
       count(r) AS num_ratings
ORDER BY num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 10;

name: Top 10 highest-rated movies
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
WITH m, count(r) AS num_ratings, avg(r.rating) AS avg_rating
WHERE num_ratings >= 150
RETURN m.movieId AS movieId,
       m.title AS title,
       num_ratings,
       round(avg_rating, 2) AS avg_rating
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC, num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 10;

name: Average rating across all movies
query:
MATCH ()-[r:RATED]->()
RETURN round(avg(r.rating), 2) AS overall_avg_rating;

name: Movies with perfect 5.0 rating and number of ratings they got
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
WITH m, count(r) AS num_ratings, avg(r.rating) AS avg_rating
WHERE avg_rating = 5.0 AND num_ratings > 1
RETURN m.movieId AS movieId,
       m.title AS title,
       num_ratings,
       round(avg_rating, 2) AS avg_rating
ORDER BY num_ratings DESC, title;

name: Movie with perfect rated
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
WITH m,
     count(r) AS num_ratings,
     avg(r.rating) AS avg_rating,
     sum(CASE WHEN r.rating = 5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS num_top_rated
WHERE num_top_rated > 0
RETURN m.movieId AS movieId,
       m.title AS title,
       num_top_rated,
       num_ratings,
       round(avg_rating, 2) AS avg_rating
ORDER BY num_top_rated DESC, num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 20;

name: Total number of users
query:
MATCH (u:User)
RETURN count(u) AS total_users;

name: Average ratings per user
Top 5 of most active user
query:
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->()
WITH u, count(r) AS num_ratings
RETURN u.userId AS userId, num_ratings
ORDER BY num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 5;

name: Last 5 least active user
query:
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->()
WITH u, count(r) AS num_ratings
RETURN u.userId AS userId, num_ratings
ORDER BY num_ratings ASC
LIMIT 5;

name: User activity distribution
query:
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->()
WITH u, count(r) AS num_ratings
WITH CASE
       WHEN num_ratings >= 1 AND num_ratings <= 20 THEN '1-20'
       WHEN num_ratings >= 21 AND num_ratings <= 50 THEN '21-50'
       WHEN num_ratings >= 51 AND num_ratings <= 100 THEN '51-100'
       WHEN num_ratings >= 101 AND num_ratings <= 200 THEN '101-200'
       ELSE '200+'
     END AS activity_bucket
RETURN activity_bucket, count(*) AS num_users
ORDER BY activity_bucket;

name: Percentage of power users (200+ ratings)
query:
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->()
WITH u, count(r) AS num_ratings
WITH count(*) AS total_users,
     sum(CASE WHEN num_ratings >= 200 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS power_users
RETURN total_users,
       power_users,
       round(100.0 * power_users / total_users, 2) AS power_user_pct;

name: Total number of unique genres
query:
MATCH (g:Genre)
RETURN count(g) AS total_genres;

name: Movies per genre
Top 9 Most Popular Genres and Their Highly Rated Movies
query:
MATCH (m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WITH g, count(DISTINCT m) AS movie_count
ORDER BY movie_count DESC
LIMIT 9

MATCH (m:Movie)-[hg:HAS_GENRE]->(g)
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m)
WITH g, hg, m, count(r) AS num_ratings, avg(r.rating) AS avg_rating
WHERE num_ratings > 50 AND avg_rating > 4.0
RETURN g, hg, m,
       round(avg_rating, 2) AS avg_rating,
       num_ratings
ORDER BY g, avg_rating DESC, num_ratings DESC;

name: Ratings per genre (total engagement)
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
RETURN g AS genre,
       count(r) AS total_ratings
ORDER BY total_ratings DESC;

name: Average rating per genre
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
RETURN g AS genre,
       round(avg(r.rating), 2) AS avg_rating,
       count(r) AS total_ratings
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC;

name: Top 5 of most popular genre
query:
MATCH (m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
RETURN g AS genre,
       count(m) AS movie_count
ORDER BY movie_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

name: Highest rated genre (by average rating)
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WITH g, avg(r.rating) AS avg_rating, count(r) AS total_ratings
WHERE total_ratings >= 50
RETURN g AS genre,
       round(avg_rating, 2) AS avg_rating,
       total_ratings
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC
LIMIT 1;

name: Multi-genre movies count (movies tagged with 3+ genres)
query:
MATCH (m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WITH m, count(g) AS genre_count
WHERE genre_count >= 3
RETURN count(m) AS multi_genre_movie_count;

name: Shared movies count between sample user pairs
query:
MATCH (u1:User {userId: 4})-[r1:RATED]->(m:Movie)<-[r2:RATED]-(u2:User {userId: 444})
RETURN u1.userId AS user1,
       u2.userId AS user2,
       count(m) AS shared_movies;

name: Cosine similarity score for sample user pairs
query:
MATCH (u1:User {userId: 4})-[r1:RATED]->(m:Movie)<-[r2:RATED]-(u2:User {userId: 444})
WITH collect(r1.rating) AS r1s, collect(r2.rating) AS r2s
WITH reduce(dot = 0.0, i IN range(0, size(r1s)-1) | dot + r1s[i] * r2s[i]) AS dot_product,
     sqrt(reduce(n1 = 0.0, x IN r1s | n1 + x * x)) AS norm1,
     sqrt(reduce(n2 = 0.0, x IN r2s | n2 + x * x)) AS norm2
RETURN round(dot_product / (norm1 * norm2), 4) AS cosine_similarity;

name: Most popular genre movie rated by users
query:
MATCH (m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WITH g, count(DISTINCT m) AS movie_count
ORDER BY movie_count DESC

MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[hg:HAS_GENRE]->(g)
WHERE r.rating >= 4.0
RETURN u, r, m, hg, g
LIMIT 80;

name: Rating distribution curve (0.5 to 5.0)
query:
MATCH ()-[r:RATED]->()
RETURN r.rating AS rating,
       count(*) AS frequency
ORDER BY rating;

name: Side-by-side comparison of total movies, users, ratings
query:
MATCH (m:Movie)
WITH count(m) AS total_movies
MATCH (u:User)
WITH total_movies, count(u) AS total_users
MATCH ()-[r:RATED]->()
RETURN total_movies, total_users, count(r) AS total_ratings;

name: Genre ranking comparison
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
RETURN g AS genre,
       count(r) AS total_ratings,
       round(avg(r.rating), 2) AS avg_rating
ORDER BY total_ratings DESC;

name: Top movie ranking comparison
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
RETURN m.movieId AS movieId,
       m.title AS title,
       count(r) AS num_ratings,
       round(avg(r.rating), 2) AS avg_rating
ORDER BY num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 20;

name: 111Top-N recommended movies for a sample user
query:
MATCH (u:User {userId: 148})-[r:RATED]->(seen:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)<-[:HAS_GENRE]-(rec:Movie)
WHERE r.rating >= 4.0
  AND NOT EXISTS {
    MATCH (u)-[:RATED]->(rec)
  }
WITH rec, count(DISTINCT g) AS shared_genres
RETURN rec.movieId AS movieId,
       rec.title AS title,
       shared_genres
ORDER BY shared_genres DESC, title
LIMIT 10;

name: 222Top-N recommended movies for a sample user1
query:
MATCH (u:User {userId: 148})-[r:RATED]->(seen:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WHERE r.rating >= 4.0
WITH u, g, count(*) AS preference_strength
MATCH (rec:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g)
WHERE NOT EXISTS {
  MATCH (u)-[:RATED]->(rec)
}
WITH rec, sum(preference_strength) AS recommendation_score
RETURN rec.movieId AS movieId,
       rec.title AS title,
       recommendation_score
ORDER BY recommendation_score DESC, title
LIMIT 10;

name: Least active user recommdiation
query:
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->()
WITH u, count(r) AS num_ratings
WHERE num_ratings = 20
WITH collect(u) AS activeUsers

MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[hg:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WHERE u IN activeUsers AND r.rating >= 4.5
RETURN u, r, m, hg, g;

name: 431 inactive user recommdiation
query:
MATCH (u:User {userId: 431})-[r:RATED]->(seen:Movie)-[sg:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WHERE r.rating >= 4.5
WITH u, g,
     collect(DISTINCT {movie: seen, ratingRel: r, genreRel: sg})[0..2] AS liked_movies,
     count(DISTINCT seen) AS preference_strength

MATCH (rec:Movie)-[hg:HAS_GENRE]->(g)
WHERE NOT EXISTS {
  MATCH (u)-[:RATED]->(rec)
}

WITH u, g, liked_movies, rec, hg, preference_strength
ORDER BY preference_strength DESC, rec.title

WITH u, g, liked_movies,
     collect(DISTINCT {movie: rec, rel: hg, score: preference_strength})[0..5] AS recs

UNWIND liked_movies AS liked
UNWIND recs AS x

RETURN u,
       liked.ratingRel,
       liked.movie,
       liked.genreRel,
       g,
       x.rel,
       x.movie

name: most active users connect through shared highly-rated movies and genres
query:
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->()
WITH u, count(r) AS num_ratings
ORDER BY num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 10
WITH collect(u) AS activeUsers

MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[hg:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WHERE u IN activeUsers AND r.rating >= 4.5

RETURN u, r, m, hg, g;

name: highly active user’s movie taste look like across genres
top 10 most-rated movies share genres with each other
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
WITH m, count(r) AS num_ratings
ORDER BY num_ratings DESC
LIMIT 10
WITH collect(m) AS topMovies

MATCH (m1:Movie)-[h1:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)<-[h2:HAS_GENRE]-(m2:Movie)
WHERE m1 IN topMovies AND m2 IN topMovies AND id(m1) < id(m2)
RETURN m1,h1, g, h2, m2;

name: genres dominate user preferences, and genre popularity comparsion
query:
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)-[:HAS_GENRE]->(g:Genre)
WITH g, count(r) AS total_ratings, avg(r.rating) AS avg_rating
ORDER BY total_ratings DESC
LIMIT 5

MATCH (m:Movie)-[hg:HAS_GENRE]->(g)
MATCH (:User)-[r:RATED]->(m)
WITH g, hg, m, count(r) AS num_ratings, avg(r.rating) AS movie_avg
WHERE num_ratings >= 20
RETURN g, hg, m, round(movie_avg, 2) AS avg_rating, num_ratings
ORDER BY g.genre, avg_rating DESC, num_ratings DESC;
